name: Dana Gioia
abstract: Michael Dana Gioia (; born December 24, 1950) is an American poet, literary critic, literary translator, and essayist. Since the early 1980s, Gioia has been considered part of the controversial and countercultural literary movements within American poetry known as New Formalism, which advocates the continued writing of poetry in rhyme an…

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: When I got the call from Dana Gioia, I was stunned... He said he wanted to recognize people who were not players... Then I accepted it
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] . . . really spending any money Dana Gioia, and that ain’t even being close to being a czar of the arts.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Dana Gioia, who is the current chairman and who’s responsible for expanding the Jazz Masters, is a terrific guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: I appreciate what Dana Gioia, the gentleman who just retired, tried to do to give us more visibility
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
